title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
text: DAGassist contains tools using directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) align regressions estimand identifying assumptions. DAGs causal graphs nonparametrically encode relationships model’s variables. good introductory articles DAGs, see Pearl (1995), Pearl (2009), Hünermund, Louw, Rönkkö (2025), Elwert (2013). DAGassist workflow five steps: (1) declare estimand; (2) draw DAG; (3) classify control variables role; (4) estimate models using DAG-consistent adjustment sets; (5) recover interpretable estimand. guide provides applied introduction DAGassist workflow.

title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
code: library(DAGassist)

title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
text: Step 1’s focus declaring estimands ensures studies maintain consistent quantity interest evaluation Lundberg, Johnson, Stewart (2021); Findley, Kikuta, Denly (2021). course, estimands may policy-relevant others Deaton (2010). purpose guide, interested sample average treatment effect (SATE).

title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
text: DAGs three basic building blocks: variables, arrows, missing arrows. DAG terminology, variables capture nodes vertices, whereas edges arcs refer arrows Tennant et al. (2021). Missing arrows equivalent strong null hypothesis. Example: Causal Effects Family Background Life Course Events Fertility Patterns purpose guide, visualize common social science question: education affect fertility Morgan Winship (2015)? DAG model encodes plausible, exhaustive, set covariates.

what: Step 3: Classify Control Variables by Role
title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
text: Interpreting roles table: X treatment / independent variable / exposure. Y outcome / dependent variable. conf stands confounder, common cause X Y. Confounders create spurious association X Y, must adjusted . med stands mediator, variable lies path X Y, transmit effect X Y. One adjust mediators one wants estimate total effect X Y. col stands collider, direct common descendant X Y. Colliders already block paths, adjusting opens spurious association X Y. dOut stands descendant outcome, descendant Y, introduces bias adjusted . dMed stands descendant mediator, adjusted estimating total effect. dCol stands descendant collider. Adjusting descendant collider opens spurious association X Y. dConfOn stands descendant confounder back door path, descendant Z affects Y. dConfOff stands descendant confounder backdoor path, decendant Z affect Y. catch-category variables fit previous definitions.
code: DAGassist(dag_model,           show="roles") ## DAGassist Report:  ##  ## Roles: ## variable         role        Exp.  Out.  conf  med  col  dOut  dMed  dCol  dConfOn  dConfOff  NCT  NCO ## edu_year         exposure    x                                                                         ## children         outcome           x                                                                   ## age              confounder              x                                                             ## class            confounder              x                                                             ## contract         confounder              x                                                             ## gender           confounder              x                                                             ## immigrant        confounder              x                                                             ## urban            confounder              x                                                             ## birth_control    mediator                      x               x                                       ## income           mediator                      x               x                                       ## job_stability_t  mediator                      x                                                       ## married          mediator                      x               x                                       ## pref             nco                                                                               x   ## religion         nco                                                                               x   ##  ## Roles legend: Exp. = exposure/treatment; Out. = outcome; CON = confounder; MED = mediator; COL = collider; dOut = descendant of outcome; dMed  = descendant of mediator; dCol = descendant of collider; dConfOn = descendant of a confounder on a back-door path; dConfOff = descendant of a confounder off a back-door path; NCT = neutral control on treatment; NCO = neutral control on outcome

what: 4. Estimate Models Using DAG-Consistent Adjustment Sets
title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
text: Interpreting model comparison table: Minimal smallest adjustment set necessary close back-door paths independent dependent variable. minimal set includes confounders controls. Canonical largest permissible adjustment set. Essentially, canonical set contains control variables confounders, mediators, intermediate outcomes, descendants mediatiors, descendants colliders. table illustrates varible roles permitted set. Note: ✓ = adjust; ✗ = adjust. may multiple minimal sets; canonical set unique.

title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
text: cases, target estimand average controlled direct effect. DAGassist supports recovering controlled direct effect using sequential g-estimation via integration DirectEffects R package. Using prior example, can use DAGassist estimate effect years education person’s number children, except birth control, income, marital status. Visualizing estimands

title: Using DAGassist for Diagnosis and Re-estimation
text: order export DAGassist reports files, users must first install commonly-used packages. Dependencies vary export file type. LaTeX uses broom fallback report generation knitr build intermediate .md Word plaintext report generation. rmarkdown convert .md files .docx files Word report generation. writexl export Excel files. Essentially, export: LaTeX needs modelsummary Excel needs modelsummary writexl plaintext needs modelsummary knitr Word needs modelsummary, knitr, rmarkdown Users can generate latex reports console (default), output file via = parameter: Word Excel output requires = parameter:
code: DAGassist(dag_model,           formula = lm(children ~ edu_year + age + class + gender +                           immigrant + urban + birth_control + income +                           married + job_stability_t + contract + pref, data = dat),           type = "latex",           out = "out/path/filename.tex") #word example DAGassist(dag_model,           formula = lm(children ~ edu_year + age + class + gender +                           immigrant + urban + birth_control + income +                           married + job_stability_t + contract + pref, data = dat),           type = "word", #or, type = "docx"           out = "out/path/filename.docx")  #excel example DAGassist(dag_model,           formula = lm(children ~ edu_year + age + class + gender +                           immigrant + urban + birth_control + income +                           married + job_stability_t + contract + pref, data = dat),           type = "excel", #or, type = "xlsx"           out = "out/path/filename.xlsx")

title: Supported Models
text: Clusters: may pass cluster variables bare name, string, one-sided formula (e.g., clusters = cat_b, "cat_b", ~cat_b). DAGassist resolves within data. Fixed/Random effects: Random-effects bars (e.g., (1 | group)) fixest FE/IV tails (| fe ...) formula preserved. Difference--Differences: notation works, interaction term must included node DAG, named exactly model, set exposure. DAGassist breaks exposure missing mismatched.

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(     dag = dag_model,     formula = estimatr::lm_robust(         ancvs_change_1116 ~ western_belt +             unemploy_broad + black_popshare + col_popshare +             indasian_popshare + other_popshare + share_formal +             share_informal + share_traditional + afrikaans_popshare +             english_popshare + isindebele_popshare + isixhosa_popshare +             sepedi_popshare + sesotho_popshare + setswana_popshare +             signlanguage_popshare + siswati_popshare + tshivenda_popshare +             xitsonga_popshare +             latitude + longitude + latitudelongitude,         data     = df,         clusters = cat_b) )

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(     dag = dag_model2,     formula = fixest::feglm(         exc_eq ~ cat4_prevalence_best + nonvprotgovt + v2x_gencs +             WINGOImputed_fill + UNintervention + majpowintervention + UNwomen +              religious + ideolleft + lgt + substantive + v2x_gencl +              Krause_wpm_neg + eqgen_binary,         data = df %>% filter(interstateonly==0, inter_intrastate==0),         family = binomial("logit"),         cluster = ~cowcode     ) )

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(   dag = dag_model,   formula = fixest::feols(     psoe ~ time * treated2 + log_pop + no_education + growthunemployment+        men_50_rs + immigration_rs + ebal_w | province,     data = df,     cluster = ~municipality) )

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(dag=dag_model,           formula = lfe::felm(               share_female ~ lag_democracy_stock_poly_95 + lag_v2xcl_rol +                    lag_v2xcl_prpty + lag_v2x_rule + lag_v2x_jucon +                   lag_v2xlg_legcon + lag_v2x_corr + lag_v2clstown +                    lag_v2xcs_ccsi + lag_v2xps_party | year + country_isocode | 0 |                    country_isocode,               data=df_cross) )

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(     dag = dag_model,     formula = lme4::glmer(         dsu_file ~ specific + lnearnings + distortion + progress + duration +             eu + japan + mexico + korea + nonoecd +              lntotdirx + lnprod + lnpolcon + active301 +             (1 | isic3_4dig),         data   = df,         family = binomial(link = "logit"),         control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")) )

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(     dag = dag_model,     formula = lmerTest::lmer(         ekloges_PERCENTAGE_May_2012_GD ~ ekloges_PERCENTAGE_2009_GD +             N_AntifaEvent_notzero2_Oc09_May12 + avg_age + log_population  +             prop_ksenoi + (1|code_perif),          data=dta))

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(   dag = dag_model,   formula = MASS::glm.nb(     protestcount ~ civilcas_percapita + sigact_percapita + log_pop + shiapop_perc       kurdpop_perc + urban + totaloilvolx + lognearestcity + feb2011 + nov2010 +       laggedprotestcount + illiterate + incomequintilelowest +        incomequintilehighest + urate + powercontinuous,     data = iraqevents_timeseries_small,     control = glm.control(maxit = 50),     link = "log") )

title: Supported Models
code: DAGassist(   dag = dag_model,   formula = stats::lm(     restraint ~ nonvio2 + prime_frat + prime_future + prime_RU + prime_UN +        oppose92coup2 + civwar + islamist + supp_sharia + active + conscript +        soldier + junoff + senoff + branch2 + train_west + train_russia +        train_china + young + sex + edu + urban_area + employed + student +        arabic + econ1 + corr1 + dem + supp_opp_parties + continue + protested +        preboutef + post_exp + mon + factor(fgov),      data=data) )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Graham Goff. Author, maintainer. Michael Denly. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Goff G, Denly M (2026). DAGassist: Test Robustness Directed Acyclic Graphs. R package version 0.2.8, https://github.com/grahamgoff/DAGassist.
code: @Manual{,   title = {DAGassist: Test Robustness with Directed Acyclic Graphs},   author = {Graham Goff and Michael Denly},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.8},   url = {https://github.com/grahamgoff/DAGassist}, }

what: Test Robustness with Directed Acyclic Graphs
title: Test Robustness with Directed Acyclic Graphs
text: --one DAG-driven robustness check. Generate publication-quality reports classify variables causal role, compare significance DAG-derived models, explicitly target estimands.

title: Test Robustness with Directed Acyclic Graphs
text: Classifies covariates causal role (confounder, mediator, collider, descendants, neutral controls, etc.). Automates reestimation models using DAG-derived adjustment sets. Targets explicit estimands facilitate transparent comparison models. Produces publication-grade reports multiple formats (LaTeX/Word/Excel/markdown/plain text + dotwhisker). Provides weight diagnostics evaluate positivity effective sample sizes.

title: Test Robustness with Directed Acyclic Graphs
text: can install DAGassist : can install development version GitHub :
code: #install.packages("DAGassist") library(DAGassist) # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("grahamgoff/DAGassist")

title: Test Robustness with Directed Acyclic Graphs
text: Simply provide dagitty() object regression call DAGassist create report classifying variables causal role, compare specified regression minimal canonical models. Optionally, users can generate visual output via dotwhisker plots:

what: Generate and/or export report that classifies nodes, compares models, and (optionally) target causal estimands. — DAGassist
title: Generate and/or export report that classifies nodes, compares models, and (optionally) target causal estimands. — DAGassist
text: DAGassist() validates DAG + model specification, classifies node roles, builds minimal canonical adjustment sets, fits comparable models, renders compact report several formats (console, LaTeX fragment, DOCX, XLSX, plain text). can also target sample-average estimands via weighting (e.g., SATE/SATT) recover sample average controlled direct effects via sequential g-estimation (e.g., SACDE).

title: Generate and/or export report that classifies nodes, compares models, and (optionally) target causal estimands. — DAGassist
code: DAGassist(   dag,   formula = NULL,   data = NULL,   exposure,   outcome,   engine = stats::lm,   labels = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   type = c("console", "latex", "word", "docx", "excel", "xlsx", "text", "txt", "dwplot",     "dotwhisker"),   show = c("all", "roles", "models"),   out = NULL,   imply = FALSE,   eval_all = FALSE,   exclude = NULL,   omit_intercept = TRUE,   omit_factors = TRUE,   bivariate = FALSE,   estimand = c("raw", "none", "SATE", "SATT", "SACDE", "SCDE"),   engine_args = list(),   weights_args = list(),   wts_omit = NULL,   auto_acde = TRUE,   acde = list(),   directeffects_args = list() )

title: Generate and/or export report that classifies nodes, compares models, and (optionally) target causal estimands. — DAGassist
text: dag dagitty object (see dagitty::dagitty()). formula Either () standard model formula Y ~ X + ..., (b) single engine call feols(Y ~ X + Z | fe, data = df, ...). engine call provided, engine, data, extra arguments automatically extracted call. data data.frame (compatible, e.g. tibble). Optional supplied via engine call formula. exposure Optional character scalar; missing/empty, inferred DAG (must unique). outcome Optional character scalar; missing/empty, inferred DAG (must unique). engine Modeling function, default stats::lm. Ignored formula single engine call (case function taken call). labels list; optional variable labels (named character vector data.frame). verbose logical (default TRUE). Controls verbosity console printer (formulas + notes). type output type. One "console" (default), "latex"/"docx"/"word", "excel"/"xlsx", "text"/"txt", plotting types "dwplot"/"dotwhisker". type = "latex", = supplied, LaTeX fragment printed console instead written disk. show character vector list; specify sections include output. One "" (default), "roles" (roles grid ), "models" (model comparison . output file path non-console types: type="latex": LaTeX fragment written (usually .tex); omitted, fragment printed console. type="text"/"txt": plain-text file written ; omitted, report printed console. type="dotwhisker"/"dwplot": image (.png) file written ; omitted, plot rendered within RStudio. type="docx"/"word": Word (.docx) file written . type="excel"/"xlsx": Excel (.xlsx) file written . Ignored type="console". imply logical; default FALSE. Controls whether roles/sets computed pruned DAG full DAG. FALSE (default): restrict DAG evaluation exposure, outcome, terms named model (prune DAG appears specification). TRUE: evaluate full DAG allow DAG-implied controls minimal/canonical sets; roles table includes DAG nodes. eval_all logical; default FALSE. TRUE, retain original RHS terms DAG nodes (e.g., fixed effects, interactions, splines) derived minimal/canonical formulas. FALSE, non-DAG RHS terms dropped derived formulas. exclude character vector list; remove neutral controls canonical set. Recognized values "nct" (drop neutral--treatment controls) "nco" (drop neutral--outcome controls). Users can supply one , e.g. exclude = c("nco", "nct"); requested variant fitted shown separate "Canon. (-...)" column console/model exports. omit_intercept logical; drop intercept rows model comparison display (default TRUE). omit_factors logical; drop factor-level rows model comparison display (default TRUE). parameter suppresses factor output; factor terms still enter regression. bivariate logical; TRUE, include bivariate (exposure-) specification comparison table addition user's original DAG-derived models (default FALSE). estimand character vector; causal estimand(s) reported columns. : "raw" (default), "SATE", "SATT", "SACDE" (alias "SCDE"), "none". "raw": naive regression fits implied supplied engine/formulas. "SATE"/"SATT": inverse-probability weighted versions comparison model (via WeightIt) target sample ATE/ATT. "SACDE"/"SCDE": DAGs mediator(s), adds sequential g-estimation columns: () unweighted sequential-g (ii) IPW-weighted sequential-g (weights estimated without conditioning mediators) target sample average controlled direct effect. engine_args Named list extra arguments forwarded engine(...). formula engine call, arguments call merged engine_args (call values take precedence). weights_args list; arguments forwarded WeightIt computing IPW weights "SATE"/"SATT" weighted SACDE refit. trim_at supplied, weights winsorized requested quantile refitting. wts_omit character vector; terms omit weighting (treatment) model even eval_all = TRUE. Useful keeping non-DAG fixed effects outcome model preventing entering propensity/weight model. auto_acde logical; TRUE (default), automates handling conflicts specifications estimand arguments. Fails gracefully helpful error users specify ACDE estimand model without mediators. acde list; options controlled direct effect workflow (estimands "SACDE"/"SCDE"). Users can override parts sequential g-estimation specification named elements: m (mediators), x (baseline covariates), z (intermediate covariates), fe (fixed-effects variables), fe_as_factor (wrap fe factor()), include_descendants (treat descendants mediators mediators). directeffects_args Named list arguments forwarded DirectEffects::sequential_g() estimand includes "SACDE" (e.g., simulation/bootstrap controls, variance estimator options).

title: Generate and/or export report that classifies nodes, compares models, and (optionally) target causal estimands. — DAGassist
text: DAGassist_report object (named list) returned invisibly file/plot outputs printed type = "console". object contains: validation List. Output validate_spec(): DAG validity + exposure/outcome checks. roles data.frame. Raw node-role flags classify_nodes(). roles_display data.frame. Roles table formatted printing/export. labels_map Named character vector. Variable → display label map used tables/plots. controls_minimal Character vector. (Legacy) One minimal adjustment set. controls_minimal_all List character vectors. minimal adjustment sets. controls_canonical Character vector. Canonical adjustment set (possibly empty). controls_canonical_excl Named list. Filtered canonical sets created exclude. conditions List. Parsed conditional statements DAG (). formulas List. User formula plus DAG-derived formula variants (minimal/canonical/etc.). models List. Fitted models formula variant (including minimal-list fits). bad_in_user Character vector. RHS terms classified mediator/collider/etc. unevaluated Character vector. Terms carried evaluated engine. unevaluated_str Character scalar. Pretty-printed version unevaluated. settings List. Print/export settings, including coef_omit show. .__data data.frame NULL. data used fit models (stored downstream helpers). file outputs (type = "latex", "docx", "xlsx", "txt", "dotwhisker"), returned object includes attribute file, normalized output path.

title: Generate and/or export report that classifies nodes, compares models, and (optionally) target causal estimands. — DAGassist
text: Engine-call parsing. formula call (e.g., feols(Y ~ X | fe, data=df)), DAGassist extracts engine function, formula, data argument, additional engine arguments directly call; merged engine/engine_args pass explicitly (call arguments win). fixest tails. engines like fixest use | denote FE/IV parts, DAGassist preserves | ... tail constructing minimal/canonical formulas (e.g., Y ~ X + controls | fe | iv(...)). Roles grid. roles table displays short headers: Exp. (exposure), . (outcome), CON (confounder), MED (mediator), COL (collider), dOut (descendant Y), dMed (descendant mediator), dCol (descendant collider), dConfOn (descendant confounder back-door path), dConfOff (descendant confounder back-door path), NCT (neutral control treatment), NCO (neutral control outcome). extra flags used () warn bad controls, (ii) build filtered canonical sets “Canonical (–NCO)” export. Bad controls. total-effect estimation, DAGassist flags bad controls variables MED, COL, dOut, dMed, dCol. warned console omitted model-comparison table. Valid confounders (pre-treatment) eligible minimal/canonical adjustment sets. Output types. console prints roles, adjustment sets, formulas (verbose), compact model comparison (using {modelsummary} available, falling back gracefully otherwise). latex writes prints LaTeX fragment can \\input{} paper — uses tabularray long tables include requested Canon. (-NCO / -NCT) variants. docx/word writes Word doc (respects options(DAGassist.ref_docx=...) set). excel/xlsx writes Excel workbook tidy tables. text/txt writes plain-text report logs/notes. dwplot/dotwhisker produces dot-whisker visualization fitted models. Dependencies. Core requires {dagitty}. Optional enhancements: {modelsummary} (pretty tables), {broom} (fallback tidying), {rmarkdown} + pandoc (DOCX), {writexl} (XLSX), {dotwhisker}/{ggplot2} plotting. Raw vs Weighted SACDE. unweighted sequential-g estimator DirectEffects uses linear regression second stage. Frisch–Waugh–Lovell theorem, implies estimand weighted conditional variance (residualized) exposure given controls—.e., regression-weighted average unit-level effects, sample-average controlled direct effect. DAGassist therefore reports raw sequential-g result weighted sequential-g refit (using WeightIt IPW weights estimated without mediators) target sample average controlled direct effect.

title: Generate and/or export report that classifies nodes, compares models, and (optionally) target causal estimands. — DAGassist
code: if (requireNamespace("dagitty", quietly = TRUE)) {   g <- dagitty::dagitty("dag { Z -> X; X -> M; X -> Y; M -> Y; Z -> Y }")   dagitty::exposures(g) <- "X"; dagitty::outcomes(g) <- "Y"   n <- 300   Z <- rnorm(n); X <- 0.8*Z + rnorm(n)   M <- 0.9*X + rnorm(n)   Y <- 0.7*X + 0.6*M + 0.3*Z + rnorm(n)   df <- data.frame(Z, X, M, Y)    # 1) Core: DAG-derived specs + engine-call parsing   r <- DAGassist(g, lm(Y ~ X + Z + M, data = df))    # 2) Target sample-average estimands via weighting (requires WeightIt)   if (requireNamespace("WeightIt", quietly = TRUE)) {     r2 <- DAGassist(g, lm(Y ~ X + Z + M, data = df), estimand = "SATE")   }    # 3) Mediator case: sequential g-estimation (requires DirectEffects)   if (requireNamespace("DirectEffects", quietly = TRUE)) {     r3 <- DAGassist(g, lm(Y ~ X + Z + M, data = df), estimand = "SACDE")   }    # 4) File export (LaTeX fragment)   # \donttest{     out <- file.path(tempdir(), "dagassist_report.tex")     DAGassist(g, lm(Y ~ X + Z + M, data = df), type = "latex", out = out)   # } }

what: flag bad controls (mediator/collider/desc of Y) among a candidate set — bad_controls_in
title: flag bad controls (mediator/collider/desc of Y) among a candidate set — bad_controls_in

title: flag bad controls (mediator/collider/desc of Y) among a candidate set — bad_controls_in
code: bad_controls_in(dag, controls, exposure, outcome)

title: flag bad controls (mediator/collider/desc of Y) among a candidate set — bad_controls_in
text: dag dagitty DAG object. controls Character vector variable names. exposure Character; exposure node name (X). outcome Character; outcome node name (Y).

title: flag bad controls (mediator/collider/desc of Y) among a candidate set — bad_controls_in
text: character vector (possibly empty) containing elements controls identified "bad controls". essentially inverse pick_minimal_controls(), returns bad controls, rather minimal/canonical set good controls

title: flag bad controls (mediator/collider/desc of Y) among a candidate set — bad_controls_in
code: d <- ggdag::dagify( Y ~ X + M + Z, M ~ X + Z, C ~ X + Y, exposure = "X", outcome = "Y") # M: mediator / Z: confounder / C: collider  # hypothetical candidate controls controls <- c("Z", "M", "C")  # Flag controls that would bias the total effect of X on Y: bad_controls_in(d, controls = c("Z","M","C"), exposure = "X", outcome = "Y") #> [1] "M" "C"  # expected: c("M", "C")  # mediator & collider are "bad controls"; Z is OK

title: Classify DAG nodes — classify_nodes
text: Labels node causal role console tabular grid. function mostly used internal helper, can used . Users encouraged alternatively use DAGassist::DAGassist(show=roles) role table specific output.

title: Classify DAG nodes — classify_nodes
code: classify_nodes(dag, exposure, outcome)

title: Classify DAG nodes — classify_nodes
text: dag dagitty DAG object. exposure Optional– inferred DAG set; character; exposure node name (Exp.). outcome Optional– inferred DAG set; character; outcome node name (.).

title: Classify DAG nodes — classify_nodes
text: data.frame one row per node columns: variable (node name) logical flags: is_exposure, is_outcome, is_confounder, is_mediator, is_collider, is_neutral_on_treatment, is_neutral_on_outcome, is_descendant_of_mediator, is_descendant_of_collider, is_descendant_of_confounder_on_bdp, is_descendant_of_confounder_off_bdp role (single primary label)

title: Classify DAG nodes — classify_nodes
text: Roles legend: Exp. = exposure . = outcome CON = confounder MED = mediator COL = collider dOut = descendant . dMed = descendant mediator, dCol = descendant collider dConfOn = descendant confounder back-door path dConfOff = descendant confounder back-door path NCT = neutral control treatment NCO = neutral control outcome

title: Classify DAG nodes — classify_nodes
code: d1 <- dagitty::dagitty("dag {X[exposure];Y[outcome] Z -> X; Z -> Y; X -> Y }")    classify_nodes(d1) #> variable  role        Exp.  Out.  conf  med  col  dOut  dMed  dCol  dConfOn  dConfOff  NCT  NCO #> X         exposure    x                                                                         #> Y         outcome           x                                                                   #> Z         confounder              x

title: DAGassist v0.2.8
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-20

title: DAGassist v0.2.8
text: Adds support SATE/SACDE estimand recovery Consolidates documentation Supports weight diagnostics Bug fixes

title: DAGassist v0.2.7
text: CRAN release: 2025-11-11

title: DAGassist v0.2.7
text: Fixed broken link vignette CRAN resubmission CRAN version.

title: DAGassist v0.2.6
text: Supports multiple exposures Detects DAG conditions like M-Bias Butterfly Bias Added neutral control (treatment outcome) descendant confounder /back-door path roles Enabled console LaTeX / markdown output Updated export functions support new expanded roles table, added legend define abbreviations Added Canonical -neutral control (treatment/outcome) comparison column options

title: DAGassist 0.2.5
text: Added dotwhisker output functionality Added support individually generating exporting roles comparison tables Added bivariate specification comparison table default Significantly enhanced vignette, article, roxygen documentation

title: DAGassist 0.2.4
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-21

title: DAGassist 0.2.4
text: Removed compare_specs() print.compare_specs(), unused causing issues. Updated DESCRIPTION reference dagitty relevant article methods.

title: DAGassist 0.2.3
text: Fixed imply logic. Previously, roles table, imply = FALSE display RHS variables, still assessed DAG-based relationships. Renamed desc(Y) IO, intermediate outcome Dropped desc(X) roles column Added dMed (descendant mediator) dCol (descendant collider) Updated documentation added vignettes

title: DAGassist 0.2.2
text: Built pkgdown site several supporting vignettes. Significantly enhanced package documentation preparation CRAN release. Trimmed exported functions. Enhanced documented engine compatibility. Added omit_factors omit_intercept parameters. Reworked imply parameter restricts evaluation variables listed RHS. Fixed cluster argument bug.

title: DAGassist 0.2.1
text: variable labels across roles + model tables (LaTeX, .docx, .xlsx, .txt). unevaluated regressors note: list RHS terms DAG, shown outputs. keep factor dummies fixed effects default. better longtblr conversion/spacing; improved dynamic column width weighting line breaking long labels. auto-escape labels neat output. cleaner summaries label support. ## Changed consolidated helpers (assist_helpers.R + export_helpers.R). improved handling nuisance vars (FEs/tails). significantly improved documentation prep CRAN release. broader validation real replication data.

title: DAGassist 0.2.0
text: Renamed main entry point dag_assist() DAGassist().

title: DAGassist 0.2.0
text: Acyclicity checks clearer validation messages validate.R. Canonical adjustment sets alongside minimal sets. Support multiple minimal adjustment sets. Export LaTeX, Word (.docx), Excel (.xlsx), plain text (.txt). export_report() helper produce publication-grade, half-page robustness report single call. New imply parameter control DAG-implied covariate additions

title: DAGassist 0.2.0
text: Console report redesigned compact, readable tables Nicer console styling color

title: DAGassist 0.2.0
text: Fixed-effects / IV parts formulas (e.g., | region + time) preserved; validation looks pre-| part.
